key_insight: REGEN recycles expert RL replay buffers to distill a generalist model with offline RL, avoiding repeated online rollouts and backward passes.
engineering_relevance: It offers a lower-compute post-training path for scaling reasoning, code, and instruction-following capabilities in large language models.
method: Reuse replay memory generated during expert RL training instead of training from multiple teachers.
Train the generalist with offline RL on this recycled experience.
Fully decouple rollout sampling from backward optimization to cut cost.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that REGEN matches MOPD accuracy across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and instruction following while using substantially lower cost.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the replay buffer, so weak or biased expert trajectories could limit generalization.
paper_title: REGEN: Replay-recycling for Expert-to-Generalist distillation with Offline Reinforcement Learning
